description: Download, parse, and analyze Azure Web App logs via Kudu API

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth login --help` for exact arguments and options.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth logout --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use to verify Azure CLI is authenticated before running download commands. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth refresh --help` for exact arguments and options.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth profiles set-default --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management. Use to verify Azure CLI login status before downloading logs.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `azlogs cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.usage_instructions: List all locally downloaded log packages. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --filter/-f to narrow results (e.g. --filter "has_merged:true"). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields. Default limit is 100.

get.help: Get details of a specific log package.
get.usage_instructions: Get metadata and details for a single log package by name. The NAME argument is the package directory name (timestamp format like 2026-02-10_09-40-16).

download.help: Download fresh logs from Azure Web App. Downloads via Kudu API, then auto-parses into JSONL and generates HTML report. Requires 'azlogs auth login' to be configured first. By default, only entries from the last 24 hours are included in the merged output. Use --since to change the window (e.g. --since 3d, --since all). The Kudu dump already includes trace logs. Use --include-kudu-trace to re-download them via a separate API call (overwrites with a fresh snapshot).

options.name: --include-kudu-trace
options.help: Download Kudu trace logs separately (already included in dump)

options.name: --since
options.help: Only include entries from this time window (e.g. 24h, 3d, 1w, all)

download.usage_instructions: Download fresh logs from an Azure Web App via Kudu API. Auto-parses into JSONL and generates an HTML report. Use --since/-s to control the time window (default 24h; accepts 3d, 1w, all). Use --include-kudu-trace only if you need to re-download trace logs separately (they are already in the dump).

parse.help: Re-parse an existing log package. Regenerates merged output and HTML report from raw log files. Use --since to filter to a time window (default: all entries).

options.name: --since
options.help: Only include entries from this time window (e.g. 12h, 3d, 1w, all)

parse.usage_instructions: Re-parse an existing package to regenerate merged JSONL and HTML report from raw log files. Use --since/-s to filter entries (default: all). Use --format to choose jsonl or csv output. The NAME argument is the package directory name.

validate.help: Validate that merged.jsonl covers every raw log line.
validate.usage_instructions: Validate that the merged JSONL output accounts for every raw log line in the package. Use after parsing to ensure no log entries were missed.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a local log package by name. Use --yes/-y to skip the confirmation prompt.

packages.usage_instructions: Log package lifecycle -- downloading fresh logs from Azure, listing/inspecting local packages, re-parsing, validating, and deleting. Each download creates a timestamped package directory containing raw logs, merged JSONL, and an HTML report.

list.help: List log entries from a package. All filtering is client-side on local JSONL data.
list.usage_instructions: List log entries from a package with client-side filtering. Use --filter/-f for field:op:value filters (e.g. level:eq:ERROR, entity:eq:app_log, service:ilike:%automation%). Multiple --filter flags combine as OR for the same field. Use --properties/-p to select specific columns. Default limit is 100.

get.help: Get a specific log entry by source file and line number.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single log entry by its source file path and line number within a package. Use to drill into a specific entry found via entries list.

entries.usage_instructions: Query and inspect parsed log entries from a downloaded package. Use to search, filter, and drill into specific log lines from the merged JSONL data.

generate.help: Generate or regenerate an HTML report for a log package. Creates report.html alongside the merged output with: - Summary metrics (entry count, error count, time span) - Issues section (grouped errors and warnings) - Entity and level breakdowns - Hourly activity histogram - Top services with error/warning badges

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Package name to generate report for

generate.usage_instructions: Generate or regenerate an HTML report for a log package. Use --open/-o to automatically open the report in the browser after generation. The report includes summary metrics, issues, entity breakdowns, hourly histograms, and service badges.

report.usage_instructions: HTML report generation for log packages. Reports include summary metrics, grouped errors/warnings, entity and level breakdowns, hourly activity histograms, and top services with badges.

usage_instructions: Use for downloading and analyzing Azure Web App logs via the Kudu API. Commands follow `azlogs <group> <action>` pattern. Specify the app with --app/-a and resource group with --resource-group/-g as global options. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p.
